title: Make release identity and self-onboard projection parity fail closed
action: Implement D1 and D3 in the onboarding engine and fixture suite. The authoritative source markers are skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and .codex-plugin/plugin.json; every required value must be numeric, readable, and equal before target mutation. Valid greenfield targets may lack .bee/onboarding.json, .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs, and project skill projections; after apply every applicable target surface for the selected distribution mode must equal the validated source tuple. Repair canonical source self-onboarding so a release-isolated source can refresh project projections rather than silently self-skipping, but prove it in isolated fixtures and do not refresh the live .agents/.claude roots in this mixed dirty tree. Keep source/target overlap and downgrade protections fail-closed.

truths: The canonical state version and both package manifest versions are readable numeric and equal before any target change
Missing unreadable or unequal authoritative source versions refuse before changing a target

A greenfield target may begin without onboarding runtime or project projections and succeeds only after .bee/onboarding.json, .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs, and distribution-applicable project projections equal the validated tuple
Fixture self-onboarding upgrades stale project projections and a second run reports up_to_date
The mixed live source tree is not broadly copied into project projection roots before release isolation

artifacts.substantive: strict release identity and source-self projection refresh without bypassing overlap safety
behavioral regression cases for mixed tuple, valid greenfield absence, stale projections, zero mutation, and idempotency

key_links: The tuple from skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs plus both package manifests is the sole version written into onboarding and compared with target runtime/projections
The self-source case refreshes fixture project projections only after release/source identity and overlap checks pass
prohibitions: Do not weaken downgrade symlink alias or source-overlap refusal
Do not delete foreign or non-bee project skills
Do not refresh the live .agents/skills or .claude/skills trees from the mixed dirty canonical tree
Do not touch unfinished wait-loop or worktree-isolation files

trace.outcome: Closed the one untested D1 angle: added plugin-first distribution-mode regression (section 10a4, 12 checks) proving the release-identity tuple gate and runtime-tuple parity hold under --plugin-source; engine unchanged (already correct), test-only.
trace.verification_evidence: BEFORE: D1 (fail-closed release tuple) and D3 (self-source project projection refresh) were implemented in the onboarding engine (readSourceReleaseIdentity + computeSkillSync self noop/sync path) and covered by suite sections 10a-10z — EXCEPT the plugin-first distribution mode. No test ran --plugin-source at all, so the D1 claim that the strict release-identity tuple gate runs BEFORE any applicable plan 'including plugin-first runs' (readSourceReleaseIdentity comment) was unproven. FAILING BEHAVIOR NAMED: a regression skipping tuple validation or runtime-tuple parity whenever project-projection sync is off (syncSkills=false) would have shipped green — the distribution-applicable surface for that mode (vendored .bee runtime + onboarding marker) had zero coverage. AFTER: added section 10a4 (test-only; engine already behaves correctly, confirmed by an isolated fixture probe before writing) with 12 checks proving (a) greenfield --plugin-source reaches the validated tuple in .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs and onboarding.json, writes NO project projection and enumerates no project target, rechecks up_to_date; (b) a mixed numeric tuple refuses under --plugin-source (blocked_no_source), vendors no .bee runtime (zero mutation), never forceable. Verify node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs -> PASS failures: 0, skipped: 1; regression node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 334 passed, 0 failed. Both .claude and .agents projection copies mirrored to the single edited file (not a broad tree refresh).
trace.verify_output:
ok - plugin-source: greenfield apply succeeds (release identity validated for the plugin-first mode)
ok - plugin-source: vendored runtime state.mjs equals the validated release tuple
ok - plugin-source: onboarding.json records the validated release tuple
ok - plugin-source: .claude/skills project projection is not written in the plugin-first mode
ok - plugin-source: .agents/skills project projection is not written in the plugin-first mode
ok - plugin-source: a mixed release tuple refuses even in the plugin-first mode
ok - plugin-source: refused apply vendors no .bee runtime (zero mutation before the tuple gate)
ok - plugin-source: a source-tuple failure is never forceable
PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1
(regression) node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 334 passed, 0 failed
